cwn_entry.cwn_title: Docfd 13.0.0: TUI multiline fuzzy document finder
cwn_entry.cwn_what:
Hi all, I am happy to announce the release of Docfd 13.0.0.
[Repo](https://github.com/darrenldl/docfd) - [Online Demo](https://demo.docfd.sh/)
(Try typing ~/list file~ to get started in demo, ~x/~ to clear search. Documentation still WIP.)
*** What Docfd is
Think interactive grep for text files, PDFs, DOCXs, etc, but word/token based instead of regex and line based, so you can search across lines easily.
Docfd aims to provide good UX via integration with common text editors and PDF viewers, so you can jump directly to a search result with a single key press.
*Interactive use*
#+attr_html: :width 80% https://github.com/darrenldl/docfd/raw/main/demo-vhs-gifs/repo.gif
*Non-interactive use*
#+attr_html: :width 80% https://github.com/darrenldl/docfd/raw/main/demo-vhs-gifs/repo-non-interactive.gif
*** Features
- Multithreaded indexing and searching - Multiline fuzzy search of multiple files - Content view pane that shows the snippet surrounding the search result selected - Text editor and PDF viewer integration - Editable command history - rewrite/plan your actions in text editor - Search scope narrowing - limit scope of next search based on current search results - Clipboard integration
*** Changes since 11.0.0
Docfd has come a long way since last announcement here, I recommend checking out the [changelog](https://github.com/darrenldl/docfd/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) if you're interested in the improvements and new features.

Hi everyone,
I’m happy to announce that ~google-drive-ocamlfuse~ 0.9.0 is now available on opam.
This release migrates from ~libfuse~ 2 to ~libfuse~ 3, allowing the package to be installed on modern Linux distributions that no longer provide ~libfuse~ 2.
#+begin_example opam update opam install google-drive-ocamlfuse #+end_example
Repositories:
- [~google-drive-ocamlfuse~](https://github.com/astrada/google-drive-ocamlfuse) - [~ocamlfuse~](https://github.com/astrada/ocamlfuse) — OCaml bindings for ~libfuse~
Best, Alessandro

We have the pleasure of celebrating the birthday of Blaise Pascal by announcing the release of OCaml version 5.5.0.
Some of the highlights in OCaml 5.5.0 are:
*** Module-dependent Functions
Modules can now be used as function arguments in a form of lightweight functors.
For instance, we can define a function for printing a map generated by the ~Map.Make~ functor:
#+begin_src ocaml let pp_map (module M: Map.S) pp_key pp_v ppf set = if M.is_empty set then Format.fprintf ppf "ø" else let pp_sep ppf () = Format.fprintf ppf ",@ " in let pp_binding ppf (k,v) = Format.fprintf ppf "@[%a@ =@ %a@]" pp_key k pp_v v in Format.fprintf ppf "@[{@ %a@ }@]" (Format.pp_print_seq ~pp_sep pp_binding) (M.to_seq set) #+end_src
We can then apply this function on a string map #+begin_src ocaml module String_map = Map.Make(String) #+end_src with #+begin_src ocaml let () = let m = String_map.of_list ["Zero", "Zero"; "One", "Un"] in let pp_str = Format.pp_print_string in Format.printf "%a@." (pp_map (module String_map) pp_str pp_str) m #+end_src
Compared to first-class modules, the type of the function ~pp_map~ #+begin_src ocaml type 'a printer = Format.formatter -> 'a -> unit val pp_map: (module M: Map.S) -> M.key printer -> 'a printer -> 'a M.t printer #+end_src is dependent over the value of the module ~S~, and thus the function can only applied over a statically known module: #+begin_src ocaml let f (): (module Map.S) = if Random.bool () then (module Map.Make(Int)) else (module Map.Make(Float)) let fail = pp_map (f ()) #+end_src #+begin_src ocaml Error: This expression has type (module M : Map.S) -> (Format.formatter -> M.key -> unit) -> (Format.formatter -> 'a -> unit) -> Format.formatter -> 'a M.t -> unit but an expression was expected of type (module Map.S) -> 'b The module M would escape its scope This function is module-dependent. The dependency is preserved when the function is passed a static module argument (module M : S) or (module M). Its argument here is not static, so the type-checker tried instead to change the function type to be non-dependent. #+end_src
*** Relocatable Compiler
A compiler installation can now be moved or copied with no risk of hard-to-debug errors due to mixing incompatible bytecode runtime interpreters.
In practice, this means that creating a local switch when there is a global switch with the same compiler version and configuration available can be done by cloning the global switch rather than recompiling the whole compiler.
This should considerably reduce the time required to create new local opam switches out-of-the-box.
*** Polymorphic Functions as Function Arguments
Higher-rank polymorphic functions can now be defined directly by using an explicit type annotation in a function argument
#+begin_src ocaml let apply_map (map: 'a 'b. ('a -> 'b) -> 'a list -> 'b list) = map string_of_int [1;2;3], map List.singleton ["x"; "y"] let _ = apply_map List.map #+end_src
Previously defining such a function required going through either a record or an object with a polymorphic field or methods
#+begin_src ocaml type map = { map: 'a 'b. ('a -> 'b) -> 'a list -> 'b list } let apply_map {map} = map string_of_int [1;2;3], map List.singleton ["x"; "y"] #+end_src
*** Search and Replace Substring Functions
The ~String~ module has been extended with many functions for searching and replacing substrings inside a string.
#+begin_src ocaml let _true = String.includes ~affix:"aba" "abbaba" let sentence = String.replace_all ~sub:"𝄽" ~by:"word" "A 𝄽 is re𝄽ed" #+end_src
The substring search is using the 2-way string matching algorithm which has the advantage of requiring constant space memory overhead independently of the needle size.
*** Generalised Local Definitions
It is now always possible to define locally a type, a class, a module type or any kind of item that can be defined globally:
#+begin_src ocaml let mandelbrot n x = let type t = Converge | Escape of int in ... match orbit n x with | Converge -> 0 | Exit_at n -> colorize n #+end_src
*** External Types
When interfacing with foreign function libraries, it is now possible to define external type
#+begin_src ocaml type int_gmp = external "mpz_t" type float_gmp = external "mpf_t" #+end_src
Compared to an abstract type definition, the external type name "mpz_t" (resp. ~mpf_t~) makes the type distinguishable from any non-abstract types or external types with a different name.
In particular, this makes FFI types better behaved when combined with Generalised Abstract Data Types (GADTs). For instance, The typechecker is able to prove that
#+begin_src ocaml let ok: (int_gmp,[` A] ) Type.eq -> _ = function _ -> . #+end_src
is a total function because the external type ~int_gmp~ is not compatible with a polymorphic variant type.
*** Warning: Abstract types in the current module
The astute reader has probably noticed in the definition above that, in OCaml 5.4.0, the typechecker does accept
#+begin_src ocaml type int_gmp let ok: (int_gmp, [` A] ) Type.eq -> _ = function _ -> . #+end_src as total.
Indeed until OCaml 5.5.0, abstract types defined in the current module #+begin_src ocaml type a type b #+end_src
were considered as unique and provably different
#+begin_src ocaml let f: 'x. (a,b) Type.eq -> 'x = function _ -> . #+end_src
However, this special rule for local definition of abstract types was very brittle. As soon as one moved outside of the current module, it was no longer possible to prove that the types were different.
#+begin_src ocaml module M = struct type a type b end let fail: 'x. (M.a,M.b) Type.eq -> 'x = function _ -> . #+end_src #+begin_example Error: This match case could not be refuted. Here is an example of a value that would reach it: Equal #+end_example
This special typechecking rule has been removed in OCaml 5.5.0. If you were relying on it, for instance, because you used an abstract type as type-level label in a GADTs, you can change your abstract type definition to a possibly private abbreviation of a polymorphic variant
#+begin_src ocaml type a = private [`A] type b = [`B] #+end_src or a (possibly private) sum type
#+begin_src ocaml type a = A type b = private B #+end_src
If you were using an abstract type as both a type-level label and a FFI type, you can now use an external type definition which will give you a provably distinct type even outside of the current module.
*** GC improvements
Some of the ongoing work to improve the pacing of the garbage collector has been integrated in OCaml 5.5.0, two of the important changes in OCaml 5.5 GC are
- the addition of a sweep-only phase at the start of major GC - the addition of an idle phase to smooth the behaviour of the GC at the start.
*** Many incremental changes
- The Windows implementation is no more reliant on Winpthreads - Around 60 new standard library functions - Around 90 various improvements - A dozen of documentation updates - Around 40 bug fixes
Please report any unexpected behaviours on the [OCaml issue tracker](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues) and post any questions or comments you might have on our [discussion forums](https://discuss.ocaml.org).
The full list of changes can be found in the full changelog.
Happy hacking, Florian Angeletti for the OCaml team.
---
*** Installation Instructions
The base compiler can be installed as an opam switch with the following commands:
#+begin_example opam update opam switch create 5.5.0 #+end_example
The source code for the release is also directly available on:
- [GitHub](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/releases/download/5.5.0/ocaml-5.5.0.tar.gz) - [OCaml archives at Inria](https://caml.inria.fr/pub/distrib/ocaml-5.5/ocaml-5.5.0.tar.gz)
**** Fine-Tuned Compiler Configuration
If you want to tweak the configuration of the compiler, you can switch to the option variant with:
#+begin_example opam update opam switch create <switch_name> ocaml-variants.5.5.0+options <option_list> #+end_example
where ~<option_list>~ is a space separated list of ~ocaml-option-*~ packages. For instance, for a ~flambda~ and ~no-flat-float-array~ switch:
#+begin_example opam switch create 5.5.0+flambda+nffa ocaml-variants.5.5.0+options ocaml-option-flambda ocaml-option-no-flat-float-array #+end_example
/Editor’s note: please read the post for the full changelog./
